title: RagaJSON Schema
description: Machine-readable schema for describing ragas of Indian classical music.

structure.description: Structural parameters of the raga (notes, patterns, etc.).

vadi.description: Vadi — principal (most prominent) note of the raga

samvadi.description: Samvadi — second most prominent note of the raga

pakad.description: Pakad — characteristic melodic phrase of the raga

time_of_day.description: Traditional time of day (Samay Chakra) for raga performance. Array of strings.

rasa.description: List of emotional associations (rasa) for the raga

time_of_day_enum.title: TimeOfDayEnum
time_of_day_enum.description: Time of day classifications for raga performance. Based on traditional Ashta Prahar system with modern equivalents.

oneOf.description: Pratah kaal - dawn period, approximately 4-7 AM
Purvaahn - morning period, approximately 7-10 AM
Madhyaahn - late morning/midday, approximately 10 AM-1 PM
Aparaahn - afternoon period, approximately 1-4 PM
Sandhya kaal - evening twilight, approximately 4-7 PM
Pradosh kaal - early night, approximately 7-10 PM
Nisha kaal - deep night, approximately 10 PM-1 AM
Ardha-ratri - late night/early dawn, approximately 1-4 AM
No specific time restriction - suitable for any time of day

season_enum.title: SeasonEnum
season_enum.description: Reference list for indicating the season or time of year traditionally associated with raga performance.

oneOf.description: Spring season
Monsoon or rainy season
Autumn season
Pre-winter or late autumn season
Winter season
Summer season
No specific season restriction; can be performed anytime

sargam_enum.title: SargamEnum
sargam_enum.description: Hindustani sargam notes using Bhatkhande notation. Machine‑friendly const values use ASCII UPPER_SNAKE; suffix _KOMAL denotes komal (flat) notes and _TIVRA denotes tivra (sharp). displayName follows Bhatkhande convention: uppercase = shuddha, lowercase = komal, 'Ma+' = tivra.

rasa_enum.title: RasaEnum
rasa_enum.description: Emotional associations (rasa) for raga: shringara, karuna, vira, etc.

oneOf.description: Romance, love, and beauty
Compassion, grief, and pity
Heroism, courage, and bravery
Peace, tranquility, and calmness
Devotion and spiritual love
Joy, mirth, and humor
Wonder, amazement, and mystery
Anger, fury, and wrath
Disgust and aversion
Fear, terror, and horror

classification_enum.title: ClassificationEnum
classification_enum.description: Hindustani-specific raga classifications. Includes traditional categories and note-count classifications (Audav/Shadav/Sampurna).

oneOf.description: A raga that uses only the notes of its parent thaat or retains its pure distinct character
A raga that borrows a foreign note (vivadi) to enhance its beauty
A raga with a crooked or zig-zag ascending/descending scale
A modern or recently created raga
A raga of regional or folk origin
A heavy or profound raga, often performed with elaborate ornamentation in vilambit (slow) tempo
A highly melodic raga known primarily for its strong emotional appeal and pleasing nature
A principal raga that gives its name to one of the parent scales (thaats)
A pentatonic raga (uses exactly 5 notes in its scale)
A hexatonic raga (uses exactly 6 notes in its scale)
A heptatonic raga (uses all 7 notes in its scale)
